name: Sierra de Alor Trail
name_original: Sierra de Alor – circular desde Olivenza
description: Hike the difficult 11.3-mile Sierra de Alor Trail, gaining 1096 feet with panoramic views and spring wildflowers in 5 hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Olivenza offers diverse natural landscapes in Extremadura, Spain, characterized by its position on the Spanish-Portuguese border. The region features the Sierra de Alor, a Special Conservation Area with elevations over 600m, providing varied terrain for exploration. Hikers can also find tranquil paths along the confluence of the Caya and Guadiana Rivers and technical single-track trails around the Embalse de Piedra Aguda. Extensive woodlands of holly oak and cork oaks, typical of the dehesa ecosystem, contribute to the area's ecological value and provide shaded routes. Best hiking trails around Olivenza The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Alor Trail, an 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation changes within the Sierra de Alor. Another top favourite among local hikers is San Jorge de Alor – Sierra de Alor Viewpoint loop from San Jorge de Alor, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.9 km) path. This loop provides views of the Sierra de Alor and surrounding natural areas. Local hikers also love the Sierra de Alor Viewpoint loop from San Jorge de Alor, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Alor, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Olivenza is defined by the Sierra de Alor mountain range, the riparian environments of the Caya and Guadiana rivers, and extensive dehesa forests. The network offers options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and more challenging climbs. The routes in Olivenza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 140 hikers have used komoot to explore Olivenza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Olivenza?
A: Olivenza offers a variety of hiking experiences, from easy walks to more challenging climbs. You'll find paths through the significant &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Alor&lt;/strong&gt;, tranquil routes along the &lt;strong&gt;Confluence of the Caya and Guadiana Rivers&lt;/strong&gt;, and shaded trails within the extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; forests of holly oak and cork oaks. There are 5 easy, 11 moderate, and 1 difficult route available.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Olivenza?
A: Yes, Olivenza has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21931256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Olivença loop from Olivenza&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.7 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Charca loop from Olivenza&lt;/a&gt; offers another accessible 7.4 km option, both providing gentle terrain and pleasant scenery.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of hikes in the Olivenza area?
A: Hiking routes around Olivenza vary in length and duration. You can find shorter walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10347162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alor Viewpoint loop from San Jorge de Alor&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.2 km and takes about 2 hours 26 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991266683/sierra-de-alor-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alor Trail&lt;/a&gt;, span 18.2 km and can take over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Olivenza?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5298324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Jorge de Alor – Sierra de Alor Viewpoint loop from San Jorge de Alor&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.9 km path offering views of the Sierra de Alor, and the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21931256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Olivença loop from Olivenza&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.7 km.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking near Olivenza?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the ecological value of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Alor&lt;/strong&gt;, witness the serene meeting point of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1577341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Caya and Guadiana Rivers&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alor Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1614952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Technical Trail Along Embalse de Piedra Aguda&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views of the reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Olivenza?
A: Spring is an excellent time to hike in Olivenza, as you can witness the blooming of rare wild peonies in the Sierra de Alor. The extensive woodlands also provide shaded trails, making them enjoyable during warmer months. The region's natural environment is appealing year-round, but spring offers unique flora.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails around Olivenza?
A: The dehesa ecosystem and riparian environments around Olivenza are known for their rich biodiversity. You can observe local flora and fauna, including various bird species, and potentially other wildlife typical of the Extremadura region, especially within the Special Conservation Area of Sierra de Alor.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Olivenza?
A: The hiking routes in Olivenza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the well-preserved natural areas, and the opportunities for both challenging mountain trails and peaceful riverside walks.

Q: Are there any specific features or challenges on the trails in Sierra de Alor?
A: The Sierra de Alor, a Special Conservation Area, offers invigorating hikes with significant elevation changes, particularly at elevations over 600m. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991266683/sierra-de-alor-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alor Trail&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult due to its length and elevation, providing a good challenge for experienced hikers.

Q: Can I find trails that pass through forests or shaded areas in Olivenza?
A: Yes, the region around Olivenza is characterized by deep woods of holly oak and cork oaks, forming extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; ecosystems. These woodlands offer plenty of shaded trails, providing a cooler and more comfortable hiking experience, especially during sunnier periods.

Q: Are there any trails near water bodies like rivers or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. You can find tranquil paths along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1577341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Caya and Guadiana Rivers&lt;/a&gt;, offering serene waterside walks. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1614952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Technical Trail Along Embalse de Piedra Aguda&lt;/a&gt; runs alongside the Piedra Aguda Reservoir, providing beautiful views of the water.
tips: Refuge of Diego Corrientes, famous bandit, who was coined the expression "he stole from the rich to give to the poor"
Located 6 km from Olivenza, is the Sierra de Alor, declared a Special Conservation Area, due to its great ecological value and its good state of conservation.

faqs.question: Where does the Sierra de Alor Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route often starting near San Jorge de Alor. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally accessible with good paths. Many hikers begin their journey from the vicinity of San Jorge de Alor.

faqs.question: Is this trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Alor Trail is a circular route, meaning it forms a loop and brings you back to your starting point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sierra de Alor Trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this trail as difficult. While some sections are flat and easy, following tracks through olive groves, the path does climb, and vegetation becomes denser. Good fitness is recommended for certain parts, especially as some areas can be exposed with little shade.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Alor Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While Komoot classifies the full route as difficult, some sources describe parts as easily accessible with pathways suitable for all fitness levels, particularly shorter, less elevated sections. If you're a beginner or hiking with family, consider exploring only the flatter initial sections or shorter segments.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse. You'll start on generally flat tracks through olive groves, then progress to climbing paths where vegetation becomes denser, featuring cork oaks. Some parts can be exposed to the sun, while others offer shade from the trees.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail is known for its rich biodiversity. In spring (late March to May), you can see rare wild peonies (Paeonia broteri), orchids, and the "Rosa de Alejandría" in bloom. The area is a designated Zone of Special Conservation, making it ideal for birdwatching and observing diverse herbaceous species. You might also spot sheep grazing in the olive groves.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the Sierra de Alor Trail?
faqs.answer: The end of winter offers the greenest views. However, spring (late March to May) is particularly stunning due to the bloom of wild peonies, orchids, and other flowers, creating a "sea of color." Be mindful that some sections can be exposed, so consider cooler months or early mornings during warmer seasons.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Sierra de Alor Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can visit the Diego Corrientes Refuge, associated with the legendary bandit. You'll also find the Sierra de Alor Viewpoint, offering panoramic views of the Llanos de Olivenza region. Look out for traditional stone shepherd's huts (chozos) and strategic points like the "Casa de Dios Corriente" and the "Fuente del Gra" with local explanations.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Alor Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through natural areas, including olive groves where sheep graze, and is a Zone of Special Conservation. While not explicitly stated, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and livestock, and to ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Sierra de Alor Trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Alor is a designated Zone of Special Conservation. Typically, trails within such areas do not require specific hiking permits or entrance fees for general access, but it's always good practice to respect local regulations and stay on marked paths to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: For the most vibrant experience, plan your hike for spring (late March to May) to witness the spectacular bloom of wild peonies and other unique flora. Be aware that some sections can be exposed, so bring adequate sun protection and water, especially on south-facing parts. Starting early can help avoid the midday sun.
